zh.lifeSummary: John Page於1510年2月21日在英國賀特福爾受洗。他和Lady Mary Agnes Tate Baroness of Chandler至少有1個女兒。他去世於1556年5月5日之前在他的家鄉，埋葬在英國賀特福爾斯坦登。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: John Page于1510年2月21日在英国賀特福爾受洗。他和Lady Mary Agnes Tate Baroness of Chandler至少有1个女儿。他去世于1556年5月5日之前在他的家乡，埋葬在英国賀特福爾斯坦登。

nameMeanings.name: Page
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and French: occupational or status name for a young servant, Middle English page, paige, Old French page (from Italian paggio, ultimately from Greek paidion, a diminutive of pais ‘boy, child’). The surname has also been established in Ireland since the 16th century. In North America, this surname is also a shortened form of the French cognate Lepage .
French Canadian (Pagé): altered form of French Paget , a diminutive of 1. Compare Pashia .
North German: metonymic occupational name for a horse dealer, from Middle Low German page ‘horse’.

variants.name: Pages
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Mario, Eduardo, Jorge, Luis, Mauricio, Ramon, Fernando, Jose, Lazaro, Pedro, Alfredo.  French Jacques, Lucien, Michel.

Catalan and southern French (Pagès): occupational name for a peasant farmer or agricultural laborer, from Catalan and Old Occitan pagès ‘peasant’ (from Late Latin pagensis, a derivative of pagus ‘village, country district’).
English: variant of Page with genitival of post-medieval excrescent s.

variants.name: Pageau
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Stephane, Alphonse, Andre, Fernand, Gisele, Jean Claude, Lucien, Philippe.

variants.name: Lepage
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Marcel, Andre, Fernand, Pierre, Raoul, Adelard, Alcide, Christophe, Lucien, Normand, Adrien.
French: occupational or status name for a young servant, a variant of Page 1, with fused masculine definite article le. Compare Lapage .
French Canadian: altered form of Pagé (see Page 2).

variants.name: Padgett
meanings: English (mainly Yorkshire): from a Middle English diminutive of page, paige ‘servant, groom’ (see Page ).

variants.name: Littlepage
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English littel ‘small’ +Middle English, Old French page ‘young servant’ (see Page ).

variants.name: Pashia
meanings: Americanized form of French Canadian Pagé (see Page 2).
History: It was Jean Baptiste Pagé from QC whose surname was changed to Pashia after he settled in MO in the 1810s. Some of his descendants bear a rare variant, Pashea.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

en.lifeSummary: John Page was christened on 21 February 1510, in Hertfordshire, England. He had at least 1 daughter with Lady Mary Agnes Tate Baroness of Chandler. He died before 5 May 1556, in his hometown, and was buried in Standon, Hertfordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: John Page a été baptisé le 21 février 1510, à Hertfordshire, Angleterre. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Lady Mary Agnes Tate Baroness of Chandler. Il est décédé avant 5 mai 1556, dans sa ville natale, et a été enterré à Standon, Hertfordshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: John Page wurde am 21. Februar 1510 in Hertfordshire, England getauft. Er hatte mit Lady Mary Agnes Tate Baroness of Chandler mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb vor 5. Mai 1556 in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Standon, Hertfordshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: John Page è stato battezzato il 21 febbraio 1510 nel Hertfordshire, England. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Lady Mary Agnes Tate Baroness of Chandler. Morì prima del 5 maggio 1556, nel sua cittadina, e fu sepolto a Standon, Hertfordshire, England.

ja.St. Mary Churchyard, Standon, Hertfordsh: イギリスハートフォードシュアイアスタンドン
ja.lifeSummary: John Pageは1510年2月21日にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアで洗礼を受けた。彼はLady Mary Agnes Tate Baroness of Chandlerとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1556年5月5日以前に故郷で死去し，イギリスハートフォードシュアイアスタンドンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John Page(은)는 1510년 2월 21일 영국 허트퍼드실르에서 태어났다. 그는 Lady Mary Agnes Tate Baroness of Chandler(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1556년 5월 5일 이전 그의 고향에서 사망했으며 영국 허트퍼드실르 스탠던에 묻혔다.

pt.21 February 1510–before 5 May 1556: 21 de fevereiro de 1510–antes 5 de maio de 1556
pt.lifeSummary: John Page foi batizado em 21 de fevereiro de 1510, em Hertfordshire, Inglaterra. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha com Lady Mary Agnes Tate Baroness of Chandler. Ele faleceu antes 5 de maio de 1556, em sua cidade natal, e foi sepultado em Standon, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: John Page был крещен 21 февраля 1510 г. в местности хиртфордшир, Англия. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Lady Mary Agnes Tate Baroness of Chandler. Он умер перед 5 май 1556 г. в его родном городе и был похоронен в местности стандон, хиртфордшир, Англия.

es.21 February 1510–before 5 May 1556: 21 de febrero de 1510–antes de 5 de mayo de 1556
es.lifeSummary: John Page se bautizó el 21 de febrero de 1510, en Hertfordshire, Inglaterra. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Lady Mary Agnes Tate Baroness of Chandler. Murió antes de 5 de mayo de 1556, en su pueblo, y fue sepultado en Standon, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra.
